articles.title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
articles.description:
The volunteer-led group says this year’s celebration will return June 6 with a parade, festival, and more Pride Month events.

The post Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston Pride For The People announces parade and festival on June 6, 2026.
The event will feature a parade, festival, and additional Pride Month activities.
Local businesses and community groups can participate, enhancing Boston's cultural diversity.

articles.title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
articles.description:
A federal judge ordered that the girl be released, and she was reportedly driven back from New York to Massachusetts by federal officials.

The post Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Federal judge ordered the release of a detained teen from Marlborough.
Teen was transported back to Massachusetts from New York by federal officials.
Release highlights ongoing immigration issues affecting local families in Boston.

articles.title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
articles.description:
"Top guys need to be your top guys, and in those kind of moments, you need them even more.”

The post With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bruins' top players are underperforming, prompting potential lineup changes.
Coach Marco Sturm emphasizes the need for key players like Pastrnak and Lindholm to step up.
A reshuffle could affect team dynamics and playoff positioning for Boston residents.

articles.title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
articles.description:
A visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, Alexis Cogne is running to raise money for the Stepping Strong Center.

The post Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss researcher, is fundraising for the Stepping Strong Center.
He is participating in a marathon to support trauma survivors.
Funds raised will directly benefit Boston's trauma rehabilitation efforts.
